reviews.review_msg: Read the textbook, and you'll do well. He is a very fair grader and allows the final to replace any lower grades in the course. Exams were fair, and lectures helped with understanding.

This professor does not go through the necessary materials required for the exams. I get it, teaching the same thing every day might be boring but it is no excuse to rush everything. We wanna go home too but we paid thousands of dollars here to study. Wish he put more time and effort. The material itself is not super difficult but he needs to try.

Gustavo is my prof for E290 it's not available, so I picked E355 His lectures are boring and disproportionate He will often speak too much and rush to finish the lecture skipping over key details in the process. The TAs aren't much help as they barely respond to emails Marking is also based on luck, depends on the quantity of writing rather quality

Gustavo is someone who knows the material well, answers questions you have, and actually cares about how well you do. The exams correspond to what he teaches in class, and if your midterm grade is bad, it gets replaced by your final exam grade if that is higher. He is very kind and is not strict. I would recommend taking him for Econ courses.

Had him for 102. Was an amazing experience and got to know dr tovar very well. He's very enthusiastic and knowledgeable about what he teaches. he makes 3 hour lectures seem like 1. At the end of the year he even let us take photos with him. Very approachable.

Would take again 100%

If you have him your in for a good class.
Very nice professor, made every concepts clear and organized. Good choice to take with
He is a good professor, new to SFU.
